raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.5,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.5,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The methodology contains consistency questions worth scrutinising: threshold selection on a validation slice drawn from the same pipeline, a single-cluster training regime paired with a two-cluster generalisation claim, and a weak logical bridge between held-out metrics and the 'reasonable robustness' assertion.","R1.3":"Concrete quantitative claims are present (precision 0.71, recall 0.63, 312 labeled incidents, ~90 s onset latency) alongside a partially supported cross-cluster generalisation claim; alignment between these numbers and the stated conclusions is a plausible load-bearing concern for an open-ended 'what do you think?' over a technical artefact.","R1.6":"The prose exhibits run-on sentences throughout, a tense inconsistency ('downstream alerting systems then consumed'), and passive-voice stacking that obscures agency; writing quality is at minimum peripherally expected under an open-ended review request."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Are you asking for methodological and technical critique of the evaluation design and claims (which would make R1.2 and R1.3 load-bearing), or primarily for feedback on the writing quality and clarity of the prose (which would make R1.6 load-bearing)?"}

rationale.R1.2: The methodology contains consistency questions worth scrutinising: threshold selection on a validation slice drawn from the same pipeline, a single-cluster training regime paired with a two-cluster generalisation claim, and a weak logical bridge between held-out metrics and the 'reasonable robustness' assertion.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete quantitative claims are present (precision 0.71, recall 0.63, 312 labeled incidents, ~90 s onset latency) alongside a partially supported cross-cluster generalisation claim; alignment between these numbers and the stated conclusions is a plausible load-bearing concern for an open-ended 'what do you think?' over a technical artefact.
rationale.R1.6: The prose exhibits run-on sentences throughout, a tense inconsistency ('downstream alerting systems then consumed'), and passive-voice stacking that obscures agency; writing quality is at minimum peripherally expected under an open-ended review request.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for methodological and technical critique of the evaluation design and claims (which would make R1.2 and R1.3 load-bearing), or primarily for feedback on the writing quality and clarity of the prose (which would make R1.6 load-bearing)?
